text: Autoregressive models generate one token at a time conditioned on prior {{c1::tokens}}.
explanation: This is the foundation of many text generation systems and language models.
usage: Used in chat, code generation, and long-form text creation.
spanish: Los modelos autoregresivos generan un token a la vez condicionado por los tokens previos.

text: {{c1::Latent diffusion}} learns to denoise a compressed latent representation rather than operating on pixels directly.
explanation: It greatly improves efficiency and scalability for image generation.
usage: Central in modern image synthesis and editing tools.
spanish: La difusión latente aprende a denoiser una representación latente comprimida en lugar de operar directamente sobre píxeles.

text: Classifier-{{c1::free guidance}} steers generation toward a condition without requiring a separate classifier model.
explanation: It is a key technique for controllable text-to-image generation.
usage: Used in image generation and controllable synthesis systems.
spanish: La guía sin clasificador orienta la generación hacia una condición sin requerir un modelo clasificador separado.

text: Temperature controls how random or deterministic the next-{{c1::token}} sampling process is.
explanation: It is one of the easiest ways to trade creativity for reliability.
usage: Used in conversation, summarization, and creative generation.
spanish: La temperatura controla qué tan aleatorio o determinista es el proceso de muestreo del siguiente token.

scenario: Generative Models: Top-k and Top-p Sampling 🎲
text: Top-k and top-p sampling limit the candidate {{c1::tokens}} considered during generation.
explanation: They help avoid low-quality or incoherent outputs while preserving flexibility.
usage: Important in text generation and dialogue systems.
spanish: Top-k y top-p limitan los tokens candidatos considerados durante la generación.

text: Generative models can be trained with objectives that do not match the {{c1::downstream}} task, causing distributional mismatch.
explanation: This mismatch is a common source of poor sample quality or weak controllability.
usage: Relevant in synthetic data generation and creative systems.
spanish: Los modelos generativos pueden entrenarse con objetivos que no coinciden con la tarea downstream, causando mismatch distributivo.
